data.title: Magna International Strengthens Governance With New Board and Committee Charters
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest update is out from Magna International ( (TSE:MG) ).

Magna International has filed a Form 6-K for March 2026, formalizing an updated governance framework that includes a newly approved Board of Directors Charter as of March 26, 2026, alongside refreshed charters for its Audit, Talent Oversight and Compensation, and Technology committees. The filing also records a Code of Conduct approved on February 12, 2026, underscoring the company’s emphasis on board stewardship, executive oversight, succession planning, and a culture of integrity and entrepreneurialism, moves that are likely aimed at reinforcing investor confidence and strengthening long-term governance across its global operations.

These governance enhancements articulate detailed board responsibilities for corporate culture, executive selection and pay, strategic planning and risk oversight, and shareholder advisory input on compensation, signalling a continued alignment with evolving regulatory and best-practice standards in North American capital markets. The inclusion of a previously approved Governance, Nominating and Sustainability Committee Charter from June 30, 2022, consolidates Magna’s broader governance architecture, indicating an ongoing effort to integrate sustainability and robust oversight into its decision-making as the automotive industry navigates technological change and heightened stakeholder scrutiny.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:MG) stock is a Hold with a C$83.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Magna International stock, see the TSE:MG Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MG Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MG is a Neutral.

The score is driven by solid financial health and cash generation plus a supportive 2026 outlook (margin expansion, strong free cash flow, buybacks). Offsetting these positives are weaker recent profitability trends, rising 2025 debt, and a notably overbought technical setup that increases near-term downside risk; valuation is reasonable but not especially cheap at ~22.8x earnings.

To see Spark’s full report on MG stock, click here.

More about Magna International

Magna International Inc. is a Canadian-based global automotive supplier headquartered in Aurora, Ontario. The company designs and manufactures a broad range of vehicle components and systems for automakers worldwide, with a longstanding focus on decentralized operations, innovation, and aligning employee and shareholder interests through equity participation and profit sharing.

data.title: Greenway Technologies Delays Annual 10-K Filing
data.description:
Greenway Technologies ( (GWTI) ) has released a notification of late filing.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Greenway Technologies, Inc. & Subsidiaries has filed a Form 12b-25, notifying investors of a delay in submitting its Form 10-K (Yearly Report) for the financial year ended December 31, 2025. The filing confirms this is a late annual report under U.S. securities rules, not a change in reporting status or structure.

The company cites its very small size and reliance on a single accountant as the primary reason for the delay in preparing the Form 10-K (Yearly Report). With only one person responsible for compiling the annual report, management states that more time is needed to complete the work without incurring unreasonable effort or extra expense.

Greenway Technologies indicates that it expects to file the delayed Form 10-K (Yearly Report) within the standard 15-calendar-day extension period allowed under SEC rules. This suggests investors should anticipate the annual report to be filed shortly after the original due date, assuming no additional complications arise.

Management does not anticipate any significant change in results of operations compared with the prior fiscal year, based on the information available at the time of the notification. As with all forward-looking statements, investors should treat these comments cautiously until the full Form 10-K (Yearly Report) and audited financial statements are made public.

The company affirms that all other required periodic reports over the past 12 months have been filed, signaling ongoing compliance with its reporting obligations. The notification is signed on behalf of Greenway Technologies by Chief Financial Officer Ransom B. Jones, underscoring management’s responsibility for timely disclosure and completion of the outstanding Form 10-K (Yearly Report).

More about Greenway Technologies

Greenway Technologies, Inc. & Subsidiaries is a very small company based in Arlington, Texas. The firm operates with lean internal resources, including a single accountant handling key financial reporting tasks for the business.

Average Trading Volume: 75,066

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $12.25M

Learn more about GWTI stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Sturm Ruger Appoints Andrew Wieland as New CFO
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Sturm Ruger & Company ( (RGR) ) has shared an update.

Sturm, Ruger & Co., Inc. announced a planned leadership transition in its finance organization, with long-serving Chief Financial Officer Tom Dineen stepping down from his role on March 31, 2026, and remaining with the company until April 30, 2026 to support continuity. Effective April 1, 2026, Andrew Wieland, a finance executive with extensive manufacturing-focused experience at Eaton Corporation, will become Senior Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, reflecting Ruger’s focus on long-term financial strength and execution of its 2030 strategic plan.

In connection with Wieland’s appointment, Ruger will enter into its customary executive severance agreement, which provides defined cash, equity vesting and medical benefits if his employment is terminated without cause or he resigns for good reason, including enhanced protections following a change in control. The board highlighted Wieland’s track record in financial discipline, strategic planning and finance transformation as critical to supporting Ruger’s growth, operational excellence and value creation for employees, customers and shareholders, while expressing gratitude for Dineen’s nearly three-decade contribution to the company’s financial foundation.

The most recent analyst rating on (RGR) stock is a Buy with a $43.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Sturm Ruger & Company stock, see the RGR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on RGR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RGR is a Neutral.

The score is mainly supported by a conservative, low-risk balance sheet and consistently positive cash generation, while being held back by materially weaker profitability and elevated earnings volatility highlighted in the latest earnings call. Technicals are constructive and add support, but valuation is constrained by negative earnings and only a modest dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on RGR stock, click here.

More about Sturm Ruger & Company

Sturm, Ruger & Co., Inc. is one of the nation’s leading manufacturers of rugged, reliable firearms for the commercial sporting market, producing nearly 800 variations across more than 40 product lines under the Ruger, Marlin and Glenfield brands. The U.S.-based company emphasizes corporate and community responsibility and positions itself as a provider of quality, innovative firearms for responsible citizens.

Steel Dynamics ( (STLD) ) has issued an announcement.

On March 25, 2026, Steel Dynamics directors Richard P. Teets, Jr. and Gabriel L. Shaheen notified the board that they intend to retire at the end of their current terms and will not stand for reelection at the May 6, 2026 annual meeting. The company said their departures, which will reduce the board from nine to seven members, did not stem from any disagreement over operations, policies or practices.

In a March 27, 2026 statement, Steel Dynamics highlighted Teets’ role as co‑founder and key architect of its growth, culture and industry reputation, and praised Shaheen’s financial acumen and strategic counsel over more than 16 years of service. The leadership change marks the exit of two long‑tenured, influential directors, prompting a leaner board structure while maintaining continuity for shareholders and other stakeholders.

Steel Dynamics, Inc. is a leading industrial metals solutions company and one of the largest domestic steel producers and metal recyclers in North America, operating facilities across the United States and in Mexico. On March 26 and 27, 2026, Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings entered into a cooperation agreement with Elliott Investment Management and launched a broad board overhaul that will take effect on March 31, 2026. The company appointed five new independent directors – Alex Cruz, Kevin A. Lansberry, Steve Pagliuca, Brian P. MacDonald and Jonathan Z. As part of the changes, CEO John W. Chidsey was named chairman and Cruz was appointed lead independent director, with the new directors also taking key roles across the audit, compensation, nominating and governance, and technology and safety committees. The move, supported by Elliott as NCLH’s largest investor and paired with customary standstill and voting commitments, signals a significant governance refresh aimed at improving execution, restoring investor confidence and driving long-term shareholder value in the cruise operator’s post-pandemic recovery phase.

data.title: Cibus Launches Underwritten Public Offering of Common Stock
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Cibus ( (CBUS) ) has shared an update.

On March 25, 2026, Cibus, Inc. entered into an underwriting agreement with BTIG, LLC for an underwritten public offering of 6,976,744 shares of its Class A common stock at $2.15 per share, with a 30-day option for the underwriter to purchase up to 1,046,511 additional shares. The offering, expected to close around March 27, 2026, is anticipated to generate approximately $13.5 million in net proceeds, or up to $16.0 million if the option is fully exercised, after underwriting discounts and expenses.

Under the agreement, BTIG will receive a 7.0% underwriting discount on gross proceeds and reimbursement of up to $150,000 in documented expenses, while Cibus has provided customary indemnification against certain Securities Act liabilities. The company, along with its directors and executive officers, also agreed to a 60-day lock-up on sales or transfers of Class A common stock without the underwriter’s consent, signaling a temporary restriction on insider share sales following the capital raise.

The most recent analyst rating on (CBUS) stock is a Sell with a $2.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Cibus stock, see the CBUS Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CBUS is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (large losses, sustained cash burn, and sharply reduced equity base). Technical indicators also show negative near-term momentum. Offsetting factors come from the earnings call, which pointed to improving execution (cost cuts, financing) and multiple regulatory/commercial catalysts, but near-term funding and profitability risks remain.

To see Spark’s full report on CBUS stock, click here.

data.title: Virtuix Expands Military Training Reach With Marine Corps Deal
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Virtuix Holdings Inc. Class A ( (VTIX) ) has issued an announcement.

On March 26, 2026, Virtuix announced that the U.S. Marine Corps Training and Education Command, via partner KBR, purchased an Omni One omni-directional treadmill system for experimentation in warfighter training and mission planning. The unit is slated for delivery to TECOM’s Integration Lab in Quantico, Virginia, in April 2026, where it will be evaluated as part of broader efforts to enhance immersive military training capabilities.

The deal extends Virtuix’s defense footprint, which already includes deployments at the U.S. Military Academy at West Point, the U.S. Air Force Academy, and Yokota Air Base, underscoring growing interest in full-body VR for operational planning and combat readiness. The company views the Marine Corps deployment as validation of its Virtual Terrain Walk technology and a potential catalyst for larger defense contracts, signaling strengthening traction across multiple branches of the U.S. military and a deeper role in next-generation simulation and training.

More about Virtuix Holdings Inc. Class A

Virtuix Inc., listed on Nasdaq as VTIX, is a manufacturer of full-body virtual reality systems serving consumer, enterprise, and defense markets. Its flagship Omni-branded omni-directional treadmills let users walk and run in 360 degrees inside games and other VR applications, while its Virtual Terrain Walk platform uses XR and AI to create immersive, geo-specific training environments for clients worldwide.

Founded on immersive simulation technologies, Virtuix has increasingly targeted defense and military training use cases, positioning its systems as tools for realistic mission planning and combat readiness. By integrating AI-driven 3D reconstruction with VR movement hardware, the company aims to deepen its foothold across multiple U.S. military branches and expand its presence in the broader defense technology ecosystem.

Average Trading Volume: 422,765

Current Market Cap: $29.34M

data.title: Source Capital’s adviser nominated for Morningstar stewardship award
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Source Capital Inc. ( (SOR) ) just unveiled an update.

On March 27, 2026, First Pacific Advisors announced it had been nominated for Morningstar’s 2026 US Awards for Investing Excellence in the Exemplary Stewardship category, recognizing the firm’s focus on acting in investors’ best interests. The nomination places FPA alongside major peers PIMCO and Vanguard and reflects Morningstar’s assessment of its investor-centric culture, alignment of manager and client interests, and strong Parent Pillar rating.

FPA leaders highlighted the firm’s decades-long commitment to stewardship, including investing alongside clients and limiting fund capacity, with some fixed income strategies closed to new institutional accounts. The recognition underscores FPA’s bid to differentiate itself in the asset management industry through disciplined risk management, capital preservation, and prioritizing investor outcomes over asset growth.

More about Source Capital Inc.

First Pacific Advisors, LP is an independent, 100% employee-owned institutional money management firm focused on value investing and capital preservation. The firm manages $33 billion as of February 28, 2025, across equity, multi-asset, and fixed income strategies offered through mutual funds, ETFs, separate accounts, collective investment trusts, and the Source Capital closed-end fund.

Its product lineup includes flagship strategies such as FPA Crescent, FPA New Income, FPA Flexible Fixed Income, Queens Road value and small-cap funds, global equity and short-duration government ETFs, and Source Capital (NYSE: SOR). FPA emphasizes capacity management, often soft-closing larger funds to protect performance and align its interests with those of long-term investors.

data.title: Planet Labs Announces Redemption of Outstanding Public Warrants
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Planet Labs PBC ( (PL) ) just unveiled an announcement.

On March 27, 2026, Planet Labs PBC announced it will redeem all of its outstanding public warrants to purchase Class A common stock that were originally issued as part of its IPO units. The move follows satisfaction of the stock-price condition in the warrant agreement, reflecting a sustained share price above the $18 threshold required to trigger redemption.

Holders may exercise their public warrants at $11.50 per share until 5:00 p.m. New York City time on April 27, 2026, after which any unexercised warrants will be void, delisted and only entitled to the $0.01 per-warrant redemption price. The action simplifies Planet’s capital structure, potentially reducing future dilution for existing shareholders while forcing warrant holders to decide between exercising or accepting the nominal cash redemption.

The most recent analyst rating on (PL) stock is a Buy with a $38.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Planet Labs PBC stock, see the PL Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, PL is a Neutral.

The score is primarily driven by improving operating momentum (positive operating and free cash flow) and strong technical uptrend. Offsetting these positives are still-severe GAAP losses and a weakened balance sheet with sharply higher leverage, while valuation remains pressured due to negative earnings. Earnings-call guidance is supportive but includes near-term margin/EBITDA pressure and demand mix/timing volatility.

The company’s mission is to image the world every day and make change visible, accessible and actionable for commercial and public users. data.title: Stratus Properties Board Approves Complete Liquidation and Dissolution
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Stratus Properties ( (STRS) ) has issued an announcement.

Stratus Properties reported results for the year ended December 31, 2025, highlighting a strategic pivot in March 2026 when its board approved a plan of complete liquidation and dissolution, with an estimated liquidating distribution range of $29.73 to $37.69 per share, subject to stockholder approval. Over 2025 and early 2026, Stratus executed significant asset sales, including Lantana Place – Retail for $57.5 million and Kingwood Place for $60.8 million, negotiated additional property sales, strengthened liquidity to $74.3 million in cash with no revolver borrowings, repurchased shares, and delivered sharply higher profitability with net income attributable to common stockholders rising to $12.0 million, or $1.47 per diluted share, despite lower revenues as it moved toward monetizing its portfolio and returning capital to investors.

The company’s 2025 performance was driven by gains on property dispositions and strong leasing operations, which lifted EBITDA to $16.6 million from $4.1 million in 2024 even as total revenue fell to $29.9 million from $54.2 million because of fewer home and land sales. Management emphasized that successful sales such as West Killeen Market, Lantana Place – Retail and Kingwood Place, combined with development progress at Holden Hills, lease-up of The Saint George and continued Amarra Villas home sales, have positioned Stratus to maximize value from its remaining assets and support the execution of its liquidation plan in a manner intended to be tax-efficient for stockholders.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Trio Petroleum Corp. ( (TPET) ) just unveiled an update.

On March 24, 2026, Trio Petroleum Corp. entered into a settlement agreement with law firm McDermott Will & Schulte LLP to resolve $392,700.23 in unpaid legal fees for past services. Under the deal, Trio agreed to satisfy the obligation by issuing 446,149 restricted common shares and to use commercially reasonable efforts to register these shares for resale with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission within specified timelines. Once the resale registration statement is filed, both parties will release each other from all liabilities and claims related to the settled fees and any related acts or expenses incurred before the settlement date, effectively closing out this legacy legal payment dispute and converting a cash obligation into equity.

More about Trio Petroleum Corp.

Trio Petroleum Corp. is a Delaware-incorporated company operating in the energy sector, focusing on petroleum exploration and related activities. An update from Hitek Global ( (HKIT) ) is now available.

On March 26, 2026, HiTek Global Inc. saw its at-the-market equity offering program halted when Maxim Group LLC, acting as sales agent, terminated their sales agreement with immediate effect under the contract terms. Before the termination, the company had already placed an aggregate 5,852,011 Class A ordinary shares through the ATM program, meaning HiTek has raised capital but lost this specific channel for incremental share issuance, potentially affecting its flexibility in tapping public markets going forward.

The company’s March 27, 2026 filing also confirms that this development is now folded into its existing SEC registration statements, ensuring the ATM activity and its cessation are reflected in the broader disclosure framework available to investors. While HiTek did not disclose the reasons for the termination, the end of the arrangement with Maxim may prompt the company to seek alternative financing structures or counterparties to support future capital-raising needs.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Braskem SA ( (BAK) ) has shared an announcement.

Braskem released its management report and financial statements for the fiscal year ended December 31, 2025, against a backdrop of subdued global growth and a pressured petrochemical cycle. The company reported that global petrochemical spreads in 2025 remained under pressure due to substantial new polyethylene and polypropylene capacity and ongoing trade tensions, particularly between the United States and China, which weighed on resin pricing and industry margins.

Operationally, Braskem’s Brazilian petrochemical complexes ran at an average utilization rate of 68% in 2025, the lowest level since 2010, reflecting weaker demand and scheduled maintenance shutdowns. Its polypropylene units in the United States and Europe also operated at historically low utilization levels for the second consecutive year, while operations at the Braskem Idesa complex in Mexico were significantly affected by its first full complex-wide maintenance shutdown, which involved a large workforce and concluded in the third quarter of 2025 before ramp-up began.

data.title: Braskem Posts Weak 4Q25 EBITDA as Brazil Approves New Chemical Industry Incentives
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Braskem SA ( (BAK) ) has issued an announcement.

Braskem reported that conditions in the global petrochemical market remained weak in the fourth quarter of 2025, with geopolitical tensions, trade frictions and seasonal effects further compressing international chemical and petrochemical spreads. The company recorded consolidated recurring EBITDA of US$109 million for 4Q25, pressured by lower utilization rates in Brazil/South America and in the United States and Europe, and ended the period with gross debt of US$9.4 billion, cash of US$2.1 billion and corporate leverage of 14.74x.

Operationally, the Brazil/South America segment saw a 6‑percentage‑point drop in average utilization due mainly to a scheduled shutdown at the Bahia complex and seasonal demand, cutting recurring EBITDA for the region to US$143 million, down 30% versus 3Q25. Utilization in the United States and Europe fell 8 percentage points amid planned outages and inventory optimization, leading to a negative recurring EBITDA of US$32 million, while Mexico rebounded from prior maintenance with a 38‑point utilization increase and recurring EBITDA of US$11 million.

For stakeholders, the quarter also brought important policy developments supporting Brazil’s chemical chain, including the December 2025 enactment of Law No. 15,294/25 creating the Special Sustainability Program for the Chemical Industry (PRESIQ) from 2027 to 2031 and the March 2026 approval of Complementary Law No. 228, which significantly raises the benefit under the Special Regime for the Chemical Industry (REIQ). These measures are framed by Braskem as essential to the long‑term competitiveness and continuity of the Brazilian chemical and petrochemical sector and may help offset the impact of the current industry downcycle on future operations.

The most recent analyst rating on (BAK) stock is a Sell with a $2.80 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Braskem SA stock, see the BAK Stock Forecast page.

Braskem S.A. is a Brazilian petrochemical company and the largest resin producer in the Americas, with shares listed in São Paulo, New York and on Latibex. The group is also a global leader in biopolymers, supplying resins and chemicals to industrial clients across Brazil/South America, the United States, Europe and Mexico, with a strong focus on large petrochemical complexes.
